title: zstd features cannot be compiled in the musl environment.
author: zzl221000
body:
```
rust: 1.95
musl env: apk add --no-cache  build-base openssl-dev curl-dev zlib-dev perl cmake clang clang-dev llvm
```

introduces the default features of zstd-sys, which causes bindgen's runtime features to be included by default. The default compilation error is as follows: 
Unable to find libclang: "the `libclang` shared library at /usr/lib/llvm21/lib/libclang.so.21.1.2 could not be opened: Dynamic loading not supported"

title: rust v1.94 build error  error: could not compile rsasl (lib) due to 1 previous error
author: liuruishan-2022
body:
error: lifetime may not live long enough
   --> /home/liuxu/.cargo/registry/src/mirrors.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn-4dc01642fd091eda/rsasl-2.2.0/src/context.rs:121:16
    |
120 | pub fn build_context<'a, 'b>(provider: &'a (dyn Provider<'b> + 'a)) -> &'a Context<'b> {
    |                      -- lifetime `'a` defined here
121 |     unsafe { &*(provider as *const dyn Provider as *const Context) }
    |                ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ cast requires that `'a` must outlive `'static`
    |
note: raw pointer casts of trait objects cannot extend lifetimes
   --> /home/liuxu/.cargo/registry/src/mirrors.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn-4dc01642fd091eda/rsasl-2.2.0/src/context.rs:121:16
    |
121 |     unsafe { &*(provider as *const dyn Provider as *const Context) }
    |                ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
    = note: this was previously accepted by the compiler but was changed recently
    = help: see <https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/141402> for more information

error: could not compile `rsasl` (lib) due to 1 previous error
warning: build failed, waiting for other jobs to finish.



After upgrading to Rust version 1.94, I encountered the above error during compilation.

title: the static-linking feature has no effect
author: brunal
body:
It's an issue since it was introduced in https://github.com/fede1024/rust-rdkafka/commit/d3879c2f29f7580da619e795b5e4883efbe5b6a8.

Instead, the CARGO_FEATURE_STATIC_EXTERNAL env flag must be set.

Additionally, I'm not sure why "/src" gets appended to the given dir, nor why this dir is set as the root.

title: Potential OOM / large allocation issue in `TopicPartitionList::with_capacity(usize)`
author: cicilzx
body:
Hi, I'm currently developing a fuzzer to test safe abstractions in Rust crates. I ran into a case where calling `TopicPartitionList::with_capacity` with a very large `usize` can trigger an out-of-memory abort under AddressSanitizer. I’m not sure whether this is expected behavior, but I wanted to report it in case a small guard or documentation note could improve robustness.

## Environment

- OS: Linux x86_64 (Ubuntu)
- Rust toolchain: `nightly-2024-12-15`
- rdkafka version: 0.39.0
- Sanitizer: AddressSanitizer (ASan)

## Minimal reproduction

```rust
extern crate rdkafka;

fn main() {
    let capacity: usize = 6294967296;
    println!("usize: {}", capacity);
    let _ = rdkafka::topic_partition_list::TopicPartitionList::with_capacity(capacity);
}
```

## Observed output (ASan)

```
=================================================================
==3045310==ERROR: AddressSanitizer: out of memory: allocator is trying to allocate 0x1dcd650000 bytes
    #0 0x5d4b409d0f0c  (/home/.../target/debug/test1+0x15cf0c) (BuildId: ...)
    #1 0x5d4b40a8974a  (/home/.../target/debug/test1+0x21574a) (BuildId: ...)

==3045310==HINT: if you don't care about these errors you may set allocator_may_return_null=1
SUMMARY: AddressSanitizer: out-of-memory ...
==3045310==ABORTING

```

## Notes

In normal usage, callers probably won’t pass capacities this large, but it seems possible to accidentally provide an unexpectedly big value (e.g., from unvalidated input or a bug upstream), which can lead to a very large allocation attempt and an OOM abort. Since the code performs a narrowing cast (`capacity as i32`) before calling the underlying C API, it might be worth considering a small guard before the cast (for example, rejecting values larger than `i32::MAX as usize`, or otherwise handling them in a predictable way), or adding a brief note in the docs that the maximum capacity is limited to `i32::MAX`. If this is considered expected behavior, please feel free to close. I just wanted to share it in case a lightweight check or clarification would make the API more robust. 

Thanks for taking a look!

title: Potential soundness issue: `BorrowedHeaders` appears constructible in safe Rust; `detach()` may segfault under ASan
author: cicilzx
body:
Hi! I'm currently developing a fuzzer to test safe abstractions in Rust crates. While testing `rdkafka`, I ran into a case where a safe sequence of calls appears to trigger a segmentation fault under AddressSanitizer (ASan).

## Environment

- OS: Linux x86_64 (Ubuntu)
- Rust toolchain: `nightly-2024-12-15`
- rdkafka version: 0.39.0
- Sanitizer: AddressSanitizer (ASan)

## Minimal reproduction

```rust
extern crate rdkafka;

fn main() {
    let headers = rdkafka::message::BorrowedHeaders;
    let headers_ref = &headers;
    let _ = headers_ref.detach();
}
```

## Build & run

```bash
ASAN_OPTIONS="detect_leaks=0" RUST_BACKTRACE=1 \
cargo +nightly-2024-12-15 rustc --bin test1 -- -Zsanitizer=address && \
./target/debug/test1
```

## Observed behavior

ASan reports a SEGV (read access) and aborts, e.g.:

```text
AddressSanitizer:DEADLYSIGNAL
=================================================================
==3044412==ERROR: AddressSanitizer: SEGV on unknown address 0x4c0000040000 (pc 0x5cffcd9dedf0 bp 0x5cffcd9dfa00 sp 0x7ffddd9201c8 T0)
==3044412==The signal is caused by a READ memory access.
SUMMARY: AddressSanitizer: SEGV
```

## Expected behavior / concern

Since the reproduction uses only safe Rust, I would expect one of the following to hold:
- `BorrowedHeaders` should not be directly constructible in safe code, **or**
- `detach()` should fail safely (e.g., return an error / panic), rather than triggering UB / a segfault.

If helpful, I’m happy to provide additional details. Thanks for taking a look!

title: ClientContext::error is called with KafkaError::Global and not KafkaError::PartitionEOF when reaching the EOF on a partition
author: rutgerclaes
body:
**Observed behavior:**

When a `BaseConsumer`, configured with `enable.partition.eof = true` and a custom `ClientContext` reaches the end of a partition, the `error` method on the context is invoked with a `KafkaError::Global` error wrapping the `RDKafkaErrorCode::PartitionEOF` error code.  

It's probably because the [`handle_error_event`](https://github.com/fede1024/rust-rdkafka/blob/cf08f6c1b8dd05a969ebe3cf478ff7b559f9c005/src/client.rs#L362) in `Client` always constructs the Global variant before passing it to the context, while the [`handle_error_event`](https://github.com/fede1024/rust-rdkafka/blob/cf08f6c1b8dd05a969ebe3cf478ff7b559f9c005/src/consumer/base_consumer.rs#L241) in `BaseConsumer` checks the code and constructs a `KafkaError::PartitionEOF` when appropriate.

**Expected behavior:**

The `ClientContext` implementation of `error` receives the `KafkaError::PartitionEOF` error variant when the partition eof is reached.

title: Windows Build is broken
author: BIHB
body:
If I run `cargo test` in a Rust project with rdkafka, I get the following error message:
```
[...]
  = note: some arguments are omitted. use `--verbose` to show all linker arguments
  = note:    Bibliothek "D:\Users\<...>\backend\target\debug\deps\api-99c334ee163d66a6.lib" und Objekt "D:\Users\<...>\backend\target\debug\deps\api-99c334ee163d66a6.exp" werden erstellt.␍
          librdkafka_sys-ff0073d016e6a9d3.rlib(rdkafka_ssl.obj) : error LNK2019: Verweis auf nicht aufgelöstes externes Symbol "SSL_get_peer_certificate" in Funktion "rd_kafka_transport_ssl_verify".␍
          D:\Users\<...>\backend\target\debug\deps\api-99c334ee163d66a6.exe : fatal error LNK1120: 1 nicht aufgelöste Externe
```

Cargo.toml:
```
[package]
name = "kafka"
version.workspace = true
edition.workspace = true
authors.workspace = true
license.workspace = true
```

```
set OPENSSL_INCLUDE_DIR=C:\Program Files\OpenSSL-Win64\include
set OPENSSL_LIB_DIR=C:\Program Files\OpenSSL-Win64\lib\VC\x64
```

I made it work with one module some days ago, but now it doesn't work again.
Can you help me please?

title: Inserting headers into &mut OwnedHeaders
author: SickMcNugget

title: SSL certificate verification fails with intermediate CA despite valid certificate chain
author: OliverGavin
body:
## Summary

rdkafka fails to verify SSL certificates when the server uses an intermediate CA that is not in the system trust store, even when the intermediate CA certificate is provided in the `ssl.ca.location` bundle. OpenSSL CLI and Java Kafka client successfully verify the same certificate chain with the same CA bundle.

## Environment

- **rdkafka version:** 0.37.0
- **OpenSSL version:** 1.1.1zc
- **OS:** Linux (AL2)

## Problem Description

We have a Kafka broker that uses a certificate chain:
1. Leaf certificate (server cert)
2. Intermediate CA 
3. Root CA

The root CA is in our trust store, but the intermediate CA is not. The server correctly provides the complete certificate chain (leaf + intermediate + root) during the TLS handshake.

## Expected Behavior

rdkafka should successfully verify the certificate chain when:
- The CA bundle (`ssl.ca.location`) contains the intermediate CA + root CA
- The server provides the complete certificate chain during TLS handshake
- OpenSSL CLI can verify the same chain with the same CA bundle

## Actual Behavior

rdkafka fails with:
```
SSL handshake failed: ssl/statem/statem_clnt.c:1921:tls_process_server_certificate 
error:1416F086:SSL routines:tls_process_server_certificate:certificate verify failed: 
broker certificate could not be verified, verify that ssl.ca.location is correctly configured
```

## Verification Steps

### 1. Server provides complete chain
```bash
openssl s_client -connect broker.example.com:9093 -showcerts | grep -c "BEGIN CERTIFICATE"
# Output: 3 (leaf + intermediate + root)
```

### 2. Certificate chain structure
```bash
openssl s_client -connect broker.example.com:9093 -showcerts </dev/null 2>/dev/null | \
  sed -n '/BEGIN CERTIFICATE/,/END CERTIFICATE/p' > server_chain.pem

openssl crl2pkcs7 -nocrl -certfile server_chain.pem | \
  openssl pkcs7 -print_certs -noout -text | grep "Subject:"
```

Output:
```
Subject: CN=broker.example.com                    # Leaf
Subject: CN=Intermediate CA                       # Intermediate
Subject: CN=Root CA                               # Root
```

### 3. OpenSSL CLI verification succeeds
```bash
# Create CA bundle with intermediate + root (no leaf)
openssl s_client -connect broker.example.com:9093 -showcerts </dev/null 2>/dev/null | \
  sed -n '/BEGIN CERTIFICATE/,/END CERTIFICATE/p' | \
  awk '/BEGIN CERT/{n++} n>=2' > ca_bundle.pem

# Verify with OpenSSL CLI - SUCCEEDS
openssl s_client -connect broker.example.com:9093 \
  -CAfile ca_bundle.pem -verify 9 -verify_return_error </dev/null 2>&1 | \
  grep "Verify return code"
# Output: Verify return code: 0 (ok)
```

### 4. Java Kafka client verification succeeds

```bash
# Create JKS truststore from CA bundle
awk 'BEGIN {n=0} /BEGIN CERTIFICATE/ {n++; file="cert-" n ".pem"} {print > file}' ca_bundle.pem
keytool -import -trustcacerts -alias intermediate -file cert-1.pem -keystore truststore.jks -storepass changeit -noprompt
keytool -import -trustcacerts -alias root -file cert-2.pem -keystore truststore.jks -storepass changeit -noprompt

# Test with Java Kafka client - SUCCEEDS (gets past SSL handshake)
kafka-console-producer.sh \
  --bootstrap-server broker.example.com:9093 \
  --topic test-topic \
  --producer-property security.protocol=SSL \
  --producer-property ssl.truststore.location=truststore.jks \
  --producer-property ssl.truststore.password=changeit \
  --producer-property ssl.endpoint.identification.algorithm=
# Result: SSL handshake succeeds, connection proceeds to authentication
```

### 5. rdkafka verification fails
```rust
let mut config = ClientConfig::new();
config.set("bootstrap.servers", "broker.example.com:9093");
config.set("security.protocol", "SASL_SSL");
config.set("ssl.ca.location", "/path/to/ca_bundle.pem");  // Same file that works with OpenSSL CLI

let producer: FutureProducer = config.create()?;
// Fails with certificate verify failed
```

Verified the file is being read correctly:
- File exists and is readable (1803 bytes)
- Contains 2 certificates (intermediate + root)
- rdkafka logs confirm file is loaded

## Comparison of Results

| Client | Certificate Chain Verification | Result |
|--------|-------------------------------|---------|
| OpenSSL CLI | ✅ Success | Verify return code: 0 (ok) |
| Java Kafka Client | ✅ Success | SSL handshake completes successfully |
| rdkafka/librdkafka | ❌ Failed | SSL handshake failed: certificate verify failed |

All three use the **same CA bundle** (intermediate + root), but only rdkafka fails.

## Root Cause Analysis

The issue appears to be that rdkafka/librdkafka does not enable OpenSSL's `X509_V_FLAG_PARTIAL_CHAIN` flag, which allows verification to succeed when an intermediate CA in the chain is trusted, even if the root CA is not directly in the trust store.

Standard OpenSSL verification requires the complete chain to a trusted root CA. However, when a server provides intermediate certificates during the TLS handshake, OpenSSL should be able to use those intermediates along with the CA bundle to build a complete trust path.

The `openssl s_client` command succeeds because it properly utilizes server-provided intermediate certificates during verification. Java's Kafka client (using JSSE) also handles this correctly. rdkafka appears to not handle this case correctly.

## Workarounds Attempted

1. ✅ **Verified CA bundle is correct** - OpenSSL CLI and Java client work
2. ✅ **Tried different bundle compositions**:
   - Root CA only: fails
   - Root + Intermediate: fails  
   - Root + Intermediate + Leaf: fails
3. ✅ **Verified file permissions and paths** - file is readable
4. ✅ **Confirmed server sends complete chain** - all 3 certs provided
5. ✅ **Tested with other Kafka clients** - Java client succeeds
6. ❌ **Disable verification** - works but not acceptable for production

## Proposed Solutions

1. **Enable `X509_V_FLAG_PARTIAL_CHAIN`** in librdkafka's SSL verification setup
2. **Expose SSL verification callback** to allow custom verification logic
3. **Improve intermediate certificate handling** during TLS handshake verification

## Minimal Reproduction

```rust
use rdkafka::config::ClientConfig;
use rdkafka::producer::FutureProducer;

fn main() {
    // Assumes:
    // - Kafka broker at broker.example.com:9093 with intermediate CA cert
    // - ca_bundle.pem contains intermediate + root CA
    
    let mut config = ClientConfig::new();
    config.set("bootstrap.servers", "broker.example.com:9093");
    config.set("security.protocol", "SSL");
    config.set("ssl.ca.location", "ca_bundle.pem");
    
    let producer: Result<FutureProducer, _> = config.create();
    // Fails with: SSL handshake failed: certificate verify failed
}
```

## Additional Context

This issue prevents connecting to Kafka brokers that use intermediate CAs not present in the system trust store, even when those intermediate CAs are explicitly provided in the CA bundle. This is a common scenario in enterprise environments with internal PKI hierarchies.

The fact that OpenSSL CLI and Java's Kafka client both succeed with the same certificate chain and CA bundle strongly suggests this is an rdkafka-specific issue with how it integrates with OpenSSL's certificate verification.

## Related Issues

- Similar issues may exist in other projects using librdkafka
- This affects any deployment using internal PKI with intermediate CAs
- Workaround of disabling certificate verification is not acceptable for production use

title: sasl2-sys replacement plan?  CVE-2024-37371
author: thomasqueirozb
body:
Apparently `krb5-src` is impacted by [CVE-2024-37371](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-8wpj-v5qv-3wf4). The repo has since been archived. `rdkafka-sys` depends on [`sasl2-sys`](https://github.com/MaterializeInc/rust-sasl), which depends on the vulnerable version of `krb5-src`.

Are there any plans to replace these libraries? TIA

Original issue: https://github.com/vectordotdev/vector/issues/24208

title: Cannot use Kafka Bootstrap Servers with `localhost:port` syntax
author: bborutenko

title: Enabling feature curl-static does not enable WITH_CURL for librdkafka
author: tillrohrmann
body: While trying to enable the OIDC feature of Kafka, I realized that enabling the curl-static feature does not enable `WITH_CURL` for librdkafka. As a consequence it is not possible to statically bundle curl when trying to enable OIDC oauthbearer. I think this is not ideal since bundling makes cross compilation easier.

title: Support for librdkafka 2.12.0
author: sukhmel
body:
Please consider release with support for librdkafka 2.12.0

It has features for working with new Kafka release 4.0.0, and several bugfixes, including ones for librdkafka 2.11.0 which is currently the latest supported one.

title: Convert testing suite to TestContainers.
author: j7nw4r
body:
## Summary

The current test suite fails due to VMware/Broadcom's deprecation of Bitnami. A Kafka source that is not affected by the Bitnami deprecation is required to restore a working test setup.

## Motivation

Testing is currently blocked. Restoring a working test setup requires a Kafka source that is not affected by the Bitnami deprecation.

## Proposal

Migrate testing to [Kafka Testcontainers](https://testcontainers.com/modules/kafka/?language=rust).

Each test compilation unit (each test file) would run its own Docker Kafka container. Multiple versions are available (Apache vs Confluent, Kraft vs Zookeeper).

The first pass would be an implementation using Apache Kraft Kafka. A later step could add a test macro that enumerates the tests over all of the necessary Kafka distributions.

This change brings in a dependency on local Docker/Podman being available for testing. The current test suite already depends on external Kafka.

https://docs.rs/testcontainers/latest/testcontainers/
https://docs.rs/testcontainers-modules/latest/testcontainers_modules/

@fede1024

title: Would be nice to be able to `collect()` into TopicPartitionList
author: blp
body:
TopicPartitionList is essentially a collection of TopicPartitionListElem. Ordinarily a Rust collection would implement FromIterator so that one could use `collect` to construct such a collection, but TopicPartitionList does not do that. It would be convenient if it did.

Thanks,

Ben.
